prompt: A hyper-realistic, documentary-style photograph within a dimly lit, eccentric tailor's workshop. An elderly, obsessive tailor with spectacles is kneeling, using a tiny needle to finish a bespoke suit on a standing male client. The suit, made of fine charcoal wool, is ridiculously, obsessively tailored. It is meticulously stitched not to the client's physique, but to mimic the precise messy wrinkles of his crumpled cotton undershirt and the bulky, rectangular outline of the smartphone and car keys in his pockets. The suit looks distorted and lumpy, a perfect map of the 'noise' of his current clothing. No text, no charts, no labels. Cinematic, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting, natural film grain, 8k resolution, captured on a Fujifilm GFX100S.
metaphor: A tailor meticulously sewing a suit to the accidental wrinkles and pocket clutter of a client's undershirt, rather than fitting the human form.
